parts.text: Hello Arun Prakash, how can I help you today?
what does my irrigation plan look like
okay what are the possible pests for my crop

Possible pests and diseases for your Groundnut crop include:

Tikka Disease (Early and Late Leaf Spot): This is a fungal disease causing circular brown spots with yellow halos on leaves, eventually leading to defoliation.

Collar Rot (Aspergillus niger): A fungal disease causing sudden wilting of seedlings, blackening and rotting of the stem at the soil level, and easily pulled-out plants with rotted roots.

Aphids (Groundnut Aphid): Small black insects that cluster on growing tips and young leaves, causing curling, puckering of leaves, stunted growth, and sticky honeydew.

White Grub (Holotrichia spp.): Larvae found in the soil that eat roots and peg zones, leading to patches of wilted plants that can be easily pulled out.
what is tikka disease and how do i handle it

{
  "intent": "pest_diagnosis",
  "crop": "Groundnut",
  "diagnosis": "Tikka Disease — Early and Late Leaf Spot (Cercospora arachidicola & Phaeoisariopsis personata)",
  "confidence": "High",
  "symptoms_matched": [
    "Circular brown spots with yellow halo on upper leaf surface (early leaf spot)",
    "Dark brown to black circular spots, no yellow halo (late leaf spot)",
    "Severely infected leaves drop prematurely causing defoliation"
  ],
  "treatment": {
    "chemical": "Mancozeb 75% WP",
    "organic": "Neem oil",
    "dosage": "2 g/litre (Mancozeb) or 5 ml/litre (Neem oil)",
    "frequency": "Every 10 days from 30 Days After Sowing (DAS)"
  },
  "spray_warning": "Heavy rain is expected on March 12th (12mm), March 15th (6mm), March 16th (8mm), March 17th (14mm), and March 18th (18mm). Avoid spraying on these dates. The safest date to spray would be March 14th.",
  "prevention": "Use resistant varieties (TAG-24, ICGV-86031). Apply gypsum 500 kg/ha at pegging to improve calcium nutrition. Maintain crop rotation and avoid planting groundnut after groundnut. Remove and plow under crop debris.",
  "summary": "Tikka disease, also known as early and late leaf spot, is a fungal disease affecting groundnuts, characterized by brown or black spots on leaves, leading to premature leaf drop and significant yield loss. To treat it, you can use Mancozeb 75% WP at a dosage of 2 g/litre, spraying every 10 days from 30 DAS. An organic alternative is Neem oil at 5 ml/litre, also sprayed every 10 days. Avoid spraying on March 12th, 15th, 16th, 17th, and 18th due to expected rain. The safest day to spray is March 14th."
}

what is the NDK for my crop ?

For your Groundnut crop, the recommended NPK is 25:50:75 kg/ha.

**Split Application:** All NPK should be applied as a basal application.
**Key Micronutrient:** Calcium is critical for pod development and should be supplied via gypsum at 400 kg/ha during the pegging stage.
